audit_methods.bandit_note: Workspace-local Bandit install did not expose a runnable entrypoint cleanly in this sandbox; dynamic testing, dependency audit, and manual code review were completed successfully.

docker_compose_ps.api: not running in Docker during audit
docker_compose_ps.dashboard: not running in Docker during audit

issues_observed_before_fix.title: Server header leakage on existing localhost process
issues_observed_before_fix.detail: Live localhost API returned both 'uvicorn' and 'AegisAI' server headers before restart.

issues_observed_before_fix.title: Missing auth throttling
issues_observed_before_fix.detail: Six invalid login attempts returned only 401 responses before the fix.

issues_observed_before_fix.title: Cross-company ingest authorization bypass
issues_observed_before_fix.detail: An hr_admin for COMP_001 was able to POST /ingest/company for COMP_002 and received 201 Created before the fix.

remediation.title: Enforced company RBAC on all ingest endpoints
Redacted raw DB error text from /health/db
Upgraded vulnerable Docker JWT dependency pin
Expanded regression coverage and configurable test base URL
Updated local dev API startup guidance to suppress server header

residual_risks.title: Existing localhost process not restarted by audit
residual_risks.detail: The runtime on :8000 may still show stale header behavior until it is restarted with --no-server-header.

residual_risks.title: Token revocation remains in-memory only
residual_risks.detail: No persistent revocation store or logout endpoint is currently implemented.

residual_risks.title: SECRET_KEY enforcement is warning-based
residual_risks.detail: Weak or default-like secrets are warned about, not rejected outside development.

residual_risks.title: Host-native startup still requires DATABASE_URL override
residual_risks.detail: .env is Docker-oriented and uses db:5432; native runs should override to localhost:5432.

immediate: Restart the native localhost API process with --no-server-header.
Rebuild any Docker image that consumes requirements.docker.txt.
Keep the updated 27-test security suite in CI and local verification.
short_term: Fail closed on weak/default SECRET_KEY values outside development.
Add logout/refresh flows and persistent token revocation.
Consider extending rate limiting to additional sensitive endpoints.
long_term: Use persistent/shared storage for throttling and revocation in multi-instance deployments.
Add a reliable local static-analysis path alongside CI.
Review MLflow exposure and authentication before any non-local deployment.
